title: Cyperus conicus
taxonomy: Cyperus conicus belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. It is a genus of plants that consists of over 700 species, and Cyperus conicus is just one of them. Cyperus conicus is further classified under the order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous plants. This classification places it within the monocot group, characterized by having one seed leaf in their embryos. The family Cyperaceae is a part of the larger monocot group, the angiosperms.

The plant is traditionally used in some cultures to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. intro: Cyperus conicus, also known as Dwarf Papyrus or Chinese Papyrus, is a perennial aquatic plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus conicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cyperus conicus is often prepared as an herbal tea, a decoction, or a poultice, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets for its therapeutic benefits.
